Alerts & notifications
Critical — act now
Downtown Funding and Records Search Search BID assessments, DDA grants, TIF reimbursements, board meetings, and financial records in one place.
Annual adopted budget — C.R.S. 29-1-101 et seq · April 10, 2026
Open the search portal →
ADA Title II deadline: April 24, 2026 — 16 days. All local government websites must meet WCAG 2.1 AA. Zero district sites are confirmed compliant on PDF documents.
HB21-1110 · 28 C.F.R. Part 35 · April 8, 2026
Open district audit dashboard →
249 of 284 metro districts have no website. CORA request is the only way a resident can access their own district's records.
EPC Assessor audit · April 2026
See district audit →
WS Districts (WSDM) serves 30+ districts on a single WordPress site. Last calendar entry: 2021. No governance docs. No financials. One compliance letter fixes all 30+ simultaneously.
Platform audit · April 2026
Draft compliance letter →
High priority
All posted PDFs likely fail WCAG 1.3.1. Meeting minutes, budgets, and audit docs are almost certainly untagged. This is the real HB21-1110 failure — not the homepage.
ADA document audit · April 2026
View ADA analysis →
0 of 8 audited sites post their service plan. C.R.S. §32-1-202 requires it. This is the foundational legal document. Not one district links it publicly.
Governance gap · §32-1-202
See audit dashboard →
Annual audit documents: publicly posted by 0 of 8 districts. Required by §32-1-106. Due to State Auditor by July 31. The clock is running.
Governance gap · §32-1-106 · due July 31
See audit dashboard →
Informational
2 CORA requests pending response. Military housing numbers and district governance records both awaiting reply. Response window is still open.
CORA tracker · April 2026
View tracker →
Wolfersberger compliance and Bellamese analysis complete. New findings ready for review on developer agreements, compliance history, and parcel-level records.
Compliance analysis · April 2026
Read analysis →
Jurisdiction finder is live. Enter any El Paso County address to find your metro districts, school district, city jurisdiction, and utilities — plain language, no login.
New · April 2026
Find my jurisdiction →
CSPD Blotter — Weekly History is live. 271 incidents pulled directly from the live blotter feed, paired with a Poisson 95% confidence band to flag statistical anomalies week to week. Tabs are full APG-pattern keyboard accessible; every chart has an aria-label.
New · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open blotter dashboard →
Lorson Ranch financial dashboard is live. Seven cross-pledged metro districts, $94.2M aggregate audited deficit, IGA money flows, and a per-home tax calculator — sourced from 2026 budgets and 2024 audits. Companion plain-language briefing available as a download.
New · Special Districts · April 2026
Open Lorson Ranch dashboard →
CBI Missing Persons form — accessible rebuild is live. Resolves all seven critical/serious findings from the ADA audit: skip link, live regions, focus management, EN/ES localization, real-time validation messaging. Drops into the existing Drupal Webform infrastructure with optional Claude API routing.
New · State · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open CBI form rebuild →
LexisNexis ADAM Program dashboard — accessible rebuild is live. Audit and reference rebuild of the missing-children dashboard: APG-pattern tabs with roving tabindex and full keyboard nav, solid amber focus rings (3:1 against any surface), darker success-green for AA contrast, and "View Poster" placeholders converted from dead anchors to real buttons.
New · Private Sector · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
Open ADAM dashboard →
Skip to complaint form
EMERGENCY? Call 911. For 24/7 crisis support, dial 988. · CCRD Office: 1-800-262-4845
CO
CRD
Colorado Civil Rights Division Department of Regulatory Agencies
1-800-262-4845

Civil Rights Complaint Intake

Tell us what
happened.
We'll route it from there.

Write in plain language. You do not need to identify the legal category of your complaint. Our intake team — and the routing model that supports them — reads every submission and directs it to the correct unit: Employment, Housing, or Public Accommodations.

→

You write, we sort. Tell us what happened in your own words. As you type, we'll show which CCRD team is most likely to handle it. A real intake worker checks the routing before anything moves. You can submit a tip without your name — but to open a formal case we'll need to contact you.

  1. 1 Describe what happened
  2. 2 Your information
  3. 3 Review & submit

Describe the incident

In your own words · No legal vocabulary required

Include who, what, when, and where if you remember. You can write as much or as little as you want — there is no minimum. If you are not ready to share details, write a short summary and an investigator will follow up.

0 characters Plain language is welcome here
Suggested routing — verify below

Which CCRD unit handles this?

Pre-selected based on your description · You can change it

Select the CCRD unit that should handle this complaint

When did this happen?

Approximate is fine · Filing deadlines depend on the date of the most recent incident

Or the most recent date if it's ongoing

Your contact information

Optional for tips · Required to open a formal investigation

We'll send confirmation and investigator updates here.

Step 2 of 2 — Review before sending

Does this look right?

We'll send this to the CCRD intake team once you confirm. You can go back and change anything before submitting.

    What we cannot help with

    CCRD enforces civil rights in employment, housing, and public accommodations. We do not handle:

    • Criminal matters (call local police)
    • Landlord-tenant disputes unrelated to discrimination
    • Federal employment (file with EEOC)
    • Fraud or consumer issues (file with the Attorney General)

    Filing deadlines

    Most complaints must be filed within 300 days of the most recent incident under CADA. Housing complaints have a one-year deadline. If your incident is older, file anyway — exceptions may apply.

    What happens next

    • You'll receive an automated confirmation within minutes
    • An intake worker reviews within 5 business days
    • If the case is something CCRD can handle, we'll contact you to begin the formal intake
    • If not, we'll point you to the right agency

    Need help filing?

    If you cannot fill out this form yourself, you can:

    • Call 1-800-262-4845 — staff will take your complaint by phone
    • Visit any CCRD office — locations at ccrd.colorado.gov
    • Have someone else file on your behalf with your written consent

    Colorado Civil Rights Division

    A unit of the Colorado Department of Regulatory Agencies (DORA). We enforce the Colorado Anti-Discrimination Act (CADA) and serve as the state's civil rights enforcement agency for employment, housing, and public accommodations.

    Contact

    1-800-262-4845 dora_ccrdintake@state.co.us ccrd.colorado.gov

    Other agencies

    EEOC (federal employment) HUD (federal housing) Colorado AG (consumer fraud)
    © 2026 State of Colorado · DORA · Civil Rights Division Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 Level AAA · ADA Title II compliant
    DEMO MODE — No data is submitted
